window_summary: This window tracks the recent topjohnwu/Magisk release stream from v25.2 through v30.7, covering major feature introductions like Zygisk API v4, magic mount rewrites, Rust migration, and support for newer Android versions.

chunks.title: Magisk v25.2
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v25.2
Tag: v25.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2022-07-21T03:15:07Z; created_at: 2022-07-21T03:13:26Z
Release notes:
Maintenance release fixing various issues.
- [MagiskInit] Fix a potential issue when stub cpio is used
- [MagiskInit] Fix reboot to recovery when stub cpio is used
- [MagiskInit] Fix sepolicy.rules symlink for rootfs devices
- [General] Better data encryption detection
- [General] Move the whole logging infrastructure into Rust
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v25.2, this maintenance release fixes various issues including a potential issue when stub cpio is used, reboot to recovery when stub cpio is used, sepolicy.rules symlink for rootfs devices, better data encryption detection, and moves the whole logging infrastructure into Rust.

chunks.title: Magisk v26.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v26.0
Tag: v26.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-04-05T18:05:58Z; created_at: 2023-04-05T18:02:17Z
Release notes:
Hey! Long time no see!
### Bumping Minimum Android Version to 6.0
Magisk's support for Android Lollipop has been pretty broken for a while without it being noticed. Also, none of the active developers of Magisk have actual hardware to run Android Lollipop. We rely on using the official Android emulator for regression testing on older platforms, however Google never shipped a Lollipop emulator image with SELinux support, leaving us with no option but to drop Lollipop support since we don't feel comfortable supporting Android Lollipop without adequate testing.
### New Magic Mount Implementation
Magic Mount, the feature that make modules modify partitions, has gone through a major rewrite. The existing implementation doesn't work well with OEMs injecting overlays into their system using `overlayfs`. The new implementation fundamentally changes how filesystem mirrors are created, giving us a more accurate clone of the unmodified filesystem.
### New `sepolicy.rule` Implementation
Magisk allows modules to provide custom SELinux patches by including the file `sepolicy.rule`. Due to the complicated nature of SELinux patching, the compatibility of this functionality has been pretty spotty; many devices are not supported. In this release, a brand new pre-init partition detection mechanism has been designed to support even more devices. Due to complicated reasons, this detection mechanism cannot be performed in a custom recovery environment.
**This means that any installation of Magisk v26+ using custom recovery will be incomplete; a subsequent re-installation through the Magisk app after booting up is required.**
### Zygisk Updates
**The new Zygisk API v4 is now live!** It comes with new features and a refined PLT function hook API. The implementaton of Zygisk has also gone through some major refactoring, including new code loading/unloading mechanisms and a new PLT function hook implementation.
Head over to the [Zygisk Module Sample](https://github.com/topjohnwu/zygisk-module-sample) repository to check out the new API and documentation!
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v26.0, the minimum Android version is bumped to 6.0, a new magic mount implementation is introduced to handle overlayfs, a new sepolicy.rule implementation with pre-init partition detection is added (requiring re-installation via the app after custom recovery), and Zygisk API v4 is introduced with new features and a refined PLT function hook API.

chunks.title: Magisk v26.1
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v26.1
Tag: v26.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-04-11T09:06:16Z; created_at: 2023-04-11T08:57:30Z
Release notes:
### Changes from v26.0
- [App] Fix crashing when revoking root permissions
- [MagiskInit] Always prefer `ext4` partitions over `f2fs` when selecting the pre-init partition
- [General] Restore module files' context/owner/group from mirror. This is a regression introduced in v26.0
(The following is the same as v26.0 release notes)
Hey! Long time no see!
### Bumping Minimum Android Version to 6.0
Magisk's support for Android Lollipop has been pretty broken for a while without it being noticed. Also, none of the active developers of Magisk have actual hardware to run Android Lollipop. We rely on using the official Android emulator for regression testing on older platforms, however Google never shipped a Lollipop emulator image with SELinux support, leaving us with no option but to drop Lollipop support since we don't feel comfortable supporting Android Lollipop without adequate testing.
### New Magic Mount Implementation
Magic Mount, the feature that make modules modify partitions, has gone through a major rewrite. The existing implementation doesn't work well with OEMs injecting overlays into their system using `overlayfs`. The new implementation fundamentally changes how filesystem mirrors are created, giving us a more accurate clone of the unmodified filesystem.
### New `sepolicy.rule` Implementation
Magisk allows modules to provide custom SELinux patches by including the file `sepolicy.rule`. Due to the complicated nature of SELinux patching, the compatibility of this functionality has been pretty spotty; many devices are not supported. In this release, a brand new pre-init partition detection mechanism has been designed to support even more devices. Due to complicated reasons, this detection mechanism cannot be performed in a custom recovery environment.
**This means that any installation of Magisk v26+ using custom recovery will be incomplete; a subsequent re-installation through the Magisk app after booting up is required.**
### Zygisk Updates
**The new Zygisk API v4 is now live!** It comes with new features and a refined PLT function hook API. The implementaton of Zygisk has also gone through some major refactoring, including new code loading/unloading mechanisms and a new PLT function hook implementation.
Head over to the [Zygisk Module Sample](https://github.com/topjohnwu/zygisk-module-sample) repository to check out the new API and documentation!
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v26.1, changes from v26.0 include fixing app crashing when revoking root permissions, always preferring ext4 partitions over f2fs for pre-init partition selection, and restoring module files' context/owner/group from mirror, which was a regression in v26.0.

chunks.title: Magisk v26.2
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v26.2
Tag: v26.2; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-08-28T08:12:12Z; created_at: 2023-08-28T08:04:28Z
Release notes:
- [MagiskBoot] Support extracting boot image from `payload.bin`
- [MagiskBoot] Support cpio files containing character files
- [MagiskBoot] Support listing cpio content
- [MagiskBoot] Directly handle AVB 1.0 signing and verification without going through Java implementation
- [Daemon] Make daemon socket a fixed path in MAGISKTMP
- [resetprop] Support printing property context
- [resetprop] Support only printing persistent properties from storage
- [resetprop] Properly support setting persistent properties bypassing property_service
- [MagiskSU] Support `-g` and `-G` options
- [MagiskSU] Support switching mount namespace to PID with `-t`
- [MagiskPolicy] Fix patching extended permissions
- [MagiskPolicy] Support more syntax for extended permissions
- [MagiskPolicy] Support printing out the loaded sepolicy rules
- [App] Support patching boot image from ROM zips
- [App] Properly preserve `boot.img` when patching Samsung firmware with `init_boot.img`
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v26.2, MagiskBoot gains support for extracting boot image from payload.bin, handling cpio files with character files, listing cpio content, and directly handling AVB 1.0 signing/verification. The daemon socket becomes a fixed path in MAGISKTMP, resetprop supports printing property context and persistent properties, MagiskSU adds -g, -G, and -t options, MagiskPolicy fixes extended permissions patching and supports printing loaded sepolicy rules, and the app supports patching boot image from ROM zips and preserving boot.img for Samsung firmware with init_boot.img.

- [General] Update toolchain that produces broken arm32 executables
- [App] Fix root service unable to bind on OnePlus devices
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v26.3, the device information detection script is fixed, BusyBox is updated to 1.36.1, the toolchain that produced broken arm32 executables is updated, and a fix is applied for root service unable to bind on OnePlus devices.

chunks.title: Magisk v26.4
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v26.4
Tag: v26.4; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2023-11-06T07:35:07Z; created_at: 2023-11-06T07:31:15Z
Release notes:
- [MagiskBoot] Don't pad zeros if signed boot image is larger
- [MagiskPolicy] Fix `genfscon` and `filename_trans`
- [MagiskPolicy] Fix bug in `libsepol`
- [Zygisk] Fix and simplify file descriptor sanitization logic
- [App] Prevent OOM when patching AP tarfiles
- [App] Fix bug in device configuration detection
- [Daemon] Fix certificate parsing of APKs
- [General] Fix logging errors from C++ code being ignored
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v26.4, MagiskBoot avoids padding zeros if signed boot image is larger, MagiskPolicy fixes genfscon, filename_trans, and a libsepol bug, Zygisk fixes and simplifies file descriptor sanitization logic, the app prevents OOM when patching AP tarfiles and fixes a bug in device configuration detection, the daemon fixes certificate parsing of APKs, and logging errors from C++ code are no longer ignored.

chunks.title: Magisk v27.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v27.0
Tag: v27.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2024-02-03T07:33:55Z; created_at: 2024-02-03T06:54:41Z
Release notes:
- [Zygisk] Introduce new code injection mechanism
- [Zygisk] Support new signature introduced in U QPR2
- [SEPolicy] Update libsepol to properly set some policy config bits
- [MagiskBoot] Support compressing `init` so Magisk is installable on devices with small boot partitions
- [ResetProp] Add new wait for property feature `resetprop -w`
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v27.0, Zygisk introduces a new code injection mechanism and supports a new signature introduced in U QPR2, SEPolicy updates libsepol to properly set policy config bits, MagiskBoot supports compressing init for devices with small boot partitions, and ResetProp adds a new wait for property feature with resetprop -w.

chunks.title: Magisk (7b81e2d2) (27005)
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk (7b81e2d2) (27005)
Tag: canary-27005; draft: False; prerelease: True; published_at: 2024-07-24T00:53:06Z; created_at: 2024-07-24T00:23:50Z
Release notes:
- [Zygisk] Update internal JNI hooking implementation
- [MagiskInit] Update preinit partition detection
## Diffs to v27.0
- [General] Support 16k page size
- [General] Add support for RISC-V
- [General] Use a minimal libc to build static executables (`magiskinit` and `magiskboot`) for smaller sizes
- [Core] Remove unnecessary mirror for magic mount
- [MagiskInit] Rewrite 2SI logic for injecting `magiskinit` as `init`
- [MagiskPolicy] Preserve sepolicy config flag after patching
- [MagiskPolicy] Optimize patching rules to reduce the amount of new rules being injected
- [DenyList] Support enforcing denylist when Zygisk is disabled
- [Resetprop] Improve implementation to workaround several property modification detections
- [Resetprop] Update to properly work with property overlays
- [App] Major internal code refactoring
- [App] Support patching Samsung firmware with images larger than 8GiB
- [App] Use user-initiated job instead of foreground services on Android 14
- [App] Support Android 13+ built-in per-app language preferences
- [MagiskBoot] Support spliting kernel images without decompression
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk canary release 27005 (7b81e2d2), Zygisk updates internal JNI hooking implementation and MagiskInit updates preinit partition detection. Diffs to v27.0 include support for 16k page size, RISC-V, minimal libc for static executables, removal of unnecessary mirror for magic mount, rewrite of 2SI logic, MagiskPolicy preserving sepolicy config flag and optimizing patching rules, DenyList supporting enforcement when Zygisk is disabled, Resetprop improvements for property modification detections and property overlays, major app internal refactoring, support for patching Samsung firmware >8GiB, user-initiated job on Android 14, Android 13+ per-app language preferences, and MagiskBoot splitting kernel images without decompression.

Diffs to v28.1 include these changes plus XZ compression support and blank file node deletion.

chunks.title: Magisk v29.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v29.0
Tag: v29.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-05-14T08:33:03Z; created_at: 2025-05-14T08:26:05Z
Release notes:
This release looks minor at the surface, however, the entire codebase has gone through significant refactoring and migration. The native code in Magisk used to be mainly C++, but several contributors and I have been steadily rewriting parts of the code in Rust since April 2022. After years of effort, the Rust-ification of the project slowly began picking up steam, and at the moment of this release, over 40% of the native code has been rewritten in Rust, with several major subsystem rewrites in the PR queue, planned to be merged for the next release.
Many might wonder, why introduce a new language to the project? My reason is actually not to reduce memory safety issues (although it is a nice side benefit), but to be able to develop Magisk using a more modern programming language. After using Rust for a while, it's clear to me that using Rust allows me to write more correct code and makes me happier compared to dealing with C++. People share the [same sentiment as I do](https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1577667445719912450.html).
## Changelog
- [General] Massive internal refactoring and code migration
- [App] Support downloading module zip files with XZ compression
- [App] Disable app animations when system animations are disabled
- [MagiskMount] Support systemlessly deleting files with modules using blank file nodes
- [MagiskInit] Redesign sepolicy patching and injection logic
- [MagiskSU] Better TTY/PTY support
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v29.0, the entire codebase has undergone significant refactoring and migration, with over 40% of native code rewritten in Rust. Changelog includes massive internal refactoring, support for downloading module zip files with XZ compression, disabling app animations when system animations are disabled, systemlessly deleting files with modules using blank file nodes, redesign of sepolicy patching and injection logic, and better TTY/PTY support in MagiskSU.

chunks.title: Magisk v30.7
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: topjohnwu/Magisk
Release: Magisk v30.7
Tag: v30.7; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-02-23T08:07:01Z; created_at: 2026-02-23T08:02:33Z
Release notes:
- [MagiskInit] Support Android 16 QPR2 sepolicy format
- [MagiskInit] Support using klogdump partition as pre-init storage
- [Zygisk] Support Android 16 QPR2 and higher
- [Zygisk] Support Android XR and some Nubia devices
- [MagiskSU] Do not drop capabilities by default, even when switching to non-root UIDs. Explicitly use the `--drop-cap` argument to drop capabilities.
- [MagiskBoot] Better lzma format detection
- [MagiskBoot] Fix various commandline argument parsing bugs
### Full Changelog: [here](https://topjohnwu.github.io/Magisk/changes.html)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release v30.7, MagiskInit supports Android 16 QPR2 sepolicy format and using klogdump partition as pre-init storage, Zygisk supports Android 16 QPR2 and higher as well as Android XR and some Nubia devices, MagiskSU no longer drops capabilities by default when switching to non-root UIDs (use --drop-cap explicitly), and MagiskBoot improves lzma format detection and fixes commandline argument parsing bugs.

questions.query_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk releases, which release introduces Zygisk API v4 with a refined PLT function hook API?

answer_support.answer_text: Zygisk API v4 is now live! It comes with new features and a refined PLT function hook API.

questions.query_text: Across the topjohnwu/Magisk releases, how does the sepolicy.rule implementation change from v26.0 to v26.1?

options.text: v26.0 introduces a new pre-init partition detection mechanism, while v26.1 always prefers ext4 partitions over f2fs for pre-init partition selection.
v26.0 removes sepolicy.rule support, while v26.1 restores it.
Both releases use the same sepolicy.rule implementation without changes.
v26.0 requires custom recovery installation, while v26.1 removes that requirement.

questions.expected_answer: v26.0 introduces a new pre-init partition detection mechanism, while v26.1 always prefers ext4 partitions over f2fs for pre-init partition selection.

answer_support.answer_text: a brand new pre-init partition detection mechanism has been designed
Always prefer ext4 partitions over f2fs when selecting the pre-init partition

questions.query_text: In the topjohnwu/Magisk release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds support for downloading module zip files with XZ compression, (2) a release that introduces Zygisk API v4, (3) a release that migrates module implementation to Rust, (4) a release that supports installing Magisk into vendor_boot partition.

answer_support.answer_text: Zygisk API v4 is now live
Support downloading module zip files with XZ compression
Support installing Magisk into vendor_boot partition
